title: Preparing 1/2 SŠ Algal Inorganic Nutrient Medium
author: Jakub Nedbal
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdzai72e
materials: Prepare all the reagents and labware.
Adjust the pH to 7.5 using KOH.

steps.title: Prepare the components listed int he following table
steps.instruction: Prepare the components listed int he following table. Make sure all constituents have been sterilized and that the deionized water has been autoclaved. Work in a sterile laminar flow hood. %s %s

steps.title: Autoclave 500 ml of deionized water...
steps.instruction: Autoclave 500 ml of deionized water in a glass clean bottle. Let it cool down.

steps.title: Work in a sterile laminar flow hood
steps.instruction: Work in a sterile laminar flow hood. Add components listed in table in STEP 1 into the autoclaved deionized water in the order listed in the table. Do not add KOH yet.

steps.title: Take a small aliquot (1
steps.instruction: Take a small aliquot (1.5 ml) of the medium into a 15 ml Falcon tube.

steps.title: Measure the pH of the medium...
steps.instruction: Measure the pH of the medium inside the Falcon tube using the pH meter.

steps.title: Add a small aliquot (5μl) of...
steps.instruction: Add a small aliquot (5μl) of 2 M KOH to the medium. Mix well by closing the bottle and shaking or turning up side down a few times.

steps.title: Repeat by going back to STEP 5
steps.instruction: Repeat by going back to STEP 5.1, until pH reaches a value of approiximatelly 7.5.

steps.title: Label the bottle (1/2 SŠ, initials, date, pH)
steps.instruction: Label the bottle (1/2 SŠ, initials, date, pH).

steps.title: Store at 4 °C in dark
steps.instruction: Store at 4 °C in dark. Warm up to room temperature each time before use. Only open in a sterile cabinet.

steps.title: This protocol describes the preparation of...
steps.instruction: This protocol describes the preparation of a medium for growth of freshwater algae on an orbital shaking incubator. The medium is to be used to dilute the growing cultures at regular intervals. The medium needs to be stored in a fridge at 4 °C and warmed up each time to room temperature to minimize the stress to the culture. References Protocol for preparing the medium using filter sterilization: Preparing 1/2 SŠ algal inorganic nutrient medium ​ ​ 1/2 SŠ inorganic nutrient medium : Recipe from the Culture Collection of Autotrophic Organisms ​ ​ Zachleder & Šetlík, 1982 : The original article introducing this medium ​
